quick_facts.label: Awarding arrangement
quick_facts.value: For the 2026 admission cycle, NCHMCT conducts the curriculum, admissions, classes, examinations and marksheets; Jawaharlal Nehru University (JNU) recognizes the results and awards the postgraduate degree. Students must also register with JNU and pay its prescribed fee.

quick_facts.label: Baseline eligibility
quick_facts.value: NCHMCT's 2026 eligibility notice specifies graduation in any discipline, including hospitality, from a recognized university. Therefore, the parent B.Sc. in Hospitality and Hotel Administration is a relevant pathway but is not the only qualifying degree.

quick_facts.label: 2026 selection route
quick_facts.value: The published 2026 cycle used an NCHMCT M.Sc. Joint Entrance Examination (JEE), followed by centralized online counselling and institute allocation based on examination merit and candidate choices; the brochure also provided a limited direct-admission route for qualifying experienced hotel-management graduates.

quick_facts.label: Published 2026 standard fee
quick_facts.value: The 2026 brochure listed semester totals of ₹63,415, ₹51,500, ₹51,500 and ₹51,500 under the standard structure. This is dated fee context: certain IHMs in specified city categories may increase only the tuition component, and institutes may separately charge items such as hostel, library, locker or other charges.

sections.heading: Eligibility: a hospitality B.Sc. is relevant, but not mandatory
paragraphs: For the NCHMCT-administered M.Sc. in Hospitality Administration, the 2026 eligibility notice requires a graduation degree in any discipline, including hospitality, from a recognized university. The 2026 information brochure further states that final-year/final-semester students who had passed all earlier papers could apply if able to submit the completed bachelor’s degree by October 31, 2026; it also stated that there was no age bar for the programme. Requirements can change by admission cycle, so applicants should treat these as 2026-cycle rules and verify the next notification.
bullets: A B.Sc. in Hospitality and Hotel Administration provides a direct academic foundation, but NCHMCT’s stated eligibility also admits graduates from non-hospitality disciplines.

The 2026 brochure described direct admission for a separate, limited category: candidates with at least two years’ work experience in a 3-star-or-higher hotel after the specified hotel-management degree, or a qualifying AICTE-approved bachelor’s degree in hotel management with at least 55% aggregate. It reserved 10% of seats at each institute for this policy.

sections.heading: Duration and study format
paragraphs: The programme is structured as four semesters over two years. NCHMCT’s academic calendar describes teaching, examination-preparation periods and semester-end examinations; it also includes pre-research exposure in Semester II. The programme description emphasizes classroom learning, self-learning, industry mentorship and counselling, with research and dissertation-oriented work.
bullets: Semester I: 17 teaching weeks, followed by a winter break, examination preparation and term-end examinations in the published academic calendar.
Semester II: 17 teaching weeks, examination preparation, end-term examinations and 10 weeks of pre-research exposure in the published calendar.

Semesters III and IV: 17 teaching weeks each in the published calendar, with examination periods; the detailed curriculum has substantial research, field-study and project work later in the programme.

sections.heading: Curriculum: hospitality management plus applied research
paragraphs: The current NCHMCT syllabus frames the degree around management capability and research competence. Semester I includes management functions and behaviour in hospitality, human-resource planning, advanced marketing management, equipment and materials management, and economics. Semester II covers strategic management, employee and management relations, business ethics, organisational behaviour and development, and financial reporting and analysis.
bullets: Semester III is research-focused: research methodology; research ethics and publication; data collection, analysis and decision-making; literature-review writing; a data-analysis practical; and a research-seminar presentation.
Semester IV includes research writing, a second data-analysis practical, an industry field study, and a 12-credit research project.

The NCHMCT admission page describes Semester III specialization options in Human Resource Management or Sales and Marketing; applicants should confirm the specialization and syllabus applicable to their own entering batch.

sections.heading: Admission: entrance-exam merit, counselling and a limited direct route
paragraphs: In the published 2026 cycle, regular admission was based on merit in NCHMCT’s M.Sc. JEE and candidates’ IHM preferences, with seat allocation through centralized online counselling. The online examination was specified as 100 multiple-choice questions covering graduate-level field questions along with general knowledge, English and numerical aptitude; the brochure specified a 0.25-mark deduction for each incorrect answer. These process details and dates are cycle-specific, not standing deadlines.
bullets: For the 2026 cycle, NCHMCT published April 24, 2026 as the online-application deadline, May 2, 2026 as the JEE date, and May 11, 2026 as the results date.
The 2026 counselling framework listed a ₹2,000 counselling registration fee and a ₹20,000 seat-acceptance fee, with the latter adjusted against first-semester fees when a seat was allotted.

NCHMCT’s notices page subsequently listed M.Sc. HA 2026 seat-vacancy information and a July 16, 2026 notice about Round-I Online Entrance Test roll numbers, showing that residual-seat activity may follow the principal schedule. Applicants should consult the latest official notice rather than rely on a prior-cycle timetable.

sections.heading: Costs: 2026 published fees, with institute and city variation
paragraphs: For 2026, NCHMCT published a common standard fee structure of ₹63,415 for Semester I and ₹51,500 for each of Semesters II–IV. This corresponds to ₹217,915 over four semesters before any separately charged institute costs. The listed structure includes one-time admission/enrolment charges, NCHM and JNU charges, tuition, examination fees and a caution deposit; it is not a complete living-cost budget.
bullets: NCHMCT stated that IHMs in Category A cities could raise the tuition component by up to 40%, while specified Category B cities could raise it by up to 24%; Category C cities had no such discretion.

The 2026 brochure states that caution money, locker fees, library fees and other charges, if applicable, are payable separately. Hostel and mess charges must be obtained from the individual institute.

For the 2026 JEE application, the published fee was ₹1,000 for General/UR and OBC-NCL applicants, ₹700 for General EWS applicants, and ₹500 for SC, ST, PwD and transgender applicants. These are historical 2026-cycle figures, not a promise for later intakes.

sections.heading: Next options: hospitality leadership, training and academic pathways
paragraphs: NCHMCT describes the programme as intended for graduates seeking enhanced opportunities in the hospitality and service industry, including training, sales and marketing, human-resource management, and faculty positions in hotel-management and tourism-management institutes. These are programme-stated pathways rather than guaranteed placement or salary outcomes.
bullets: Industry pathway: pursue operational-management, sales/marketing, human-resources, training or service-sector roles, building on the programme’s management, finance, marketing and research modules.

Academic pathway: NCHMCT’s NHTET eligibility rules recognize a qualifying full-time master’s degree in Hospitality Administration, Hotel Management or Culinary Art with at least 55% marks as one route to sit the test, subject to the rule’s bachelor’s/diploma background conditions and other requirements.

Research pathway: the later semesters include research methodology, research ethics, data analysis, field study and a major research project, providing preparation for research-oriented higher study subject to the admissions rules of the prospective institution.
